question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the AUTO sourceType logic in CompositeBlockSource — where switching between BLOCK_NODE and FILE sources is based on SourceHealth.isHealthy() (errors < 3) — by causing exactly 3 errors on both sources simultaneously (e.g., via network disruption), leaving the importer stuck on an unhealthy source with no valid fallback and halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker register a large number of fake block nodes (via public Hedera NodeCreate transactions) with autoDiscoveryEnabled=true, causing BlockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() to return a massive list, exhausting memory or connection pool resources in BlockNodeSubscriber and halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: isAutoDiscoveryEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the cache invalidation mechanism in BlockNodeDiscoveryService (triggered by RegisteredNodeChangedEvent) by rapidly submitting NodeCreate/NodeUpdate/NodeDelete transactions on-chain, causing the cache to be invalidated and rebuilt continuously, creating a thundering herd of database queries that exhausts DB connections and halts block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set stream.maxSubscribeAttempts to Integer.MAX_VALUE via environment variable injection, causing BlockNodeSubscriber to retry a permanently-failed block node indefinitely without ever marking it inactive or switching to an alternative source, halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the case-insensitive network name handling in ImporterProperties.getNetwork() (which calls toLowerCase()) to cause getBucketName() to return an empty string by injecting a network name that does not match any key in NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS, resulting in S3StreamFileProvider attempting to list objects in a bucket with an empty name and failing all block file downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform a DNS rebinding attack against the host values in BlockNodeProperties within nodes, initially resolving to a legitimate block node IP but then rebinding to an internal network address, causing the importer to connect to unintended internal services, receive malformed responses, and halt block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who operates a malicious block node (registered via a public Hedera NodeCreate transaction) send streaming responses that are exactly at the stream.maxStreamResponseSize limit but contain only partial block items, causing BlockNodeSubscriber to buffer indefinitely waiting for a BlockProof item that never arrives, halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who operates a malicious block node send exactly stream.maxBlockItems - 1 block items per response without ever including a BlockProof, causing BlockNodeSubscriber to accumulate items indefinitely in memory, leading to OOM errors that crash the importer and halt all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that LedgerProperties.nodeContributions defaults to an empty list (List.of()) when ledger is configured without contributions, causing TSS verification to fail due to insufficient node contributions, rejecting all blocks and halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform a TLS downgrade attack against block node connections when requiresTls is false (the default in BlockNodeProperties), intercepting the plaintext gRPC stream and injecting crafted block items that cause verification failures, halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName) check in getBucketName() by injecting a bucketName value consisting only of whitespace characters (which isNotBlank would treat as blank), causing the fallback to getBlockStreamBucketName() to return an empty string for an unknown network, and halting all block file downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the Spring Boot @ConfigurationProperties binding for frequency by injecting a malformed Duration string (e.g., 'PT0S' for zero duration) that passes @NotNull validation but causes the @Scheduled fixedDelay to be zero, creating a tight loop that starves all other threads and halts block processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can submit Hedera NodeUpdate transactions modify the service endpoints of existing block nodes to point to attacker-controlled hosts, causing BlockNodeDiscoveryService to return attacker-controlled nodes (since auto-discovered properties override config file properties when mergeKey matches), and then serve malformed blocks that halt ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set stream.readmitDelay to exactly 10 seconds (the @DurationMin minimum) via environment variable injection, and then repeatedly cause block node errors every 9 seconds, preventing nodes from ever being successfully readmitted and keeping all nodes in an inactive state, halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that sourceType defaults to BlockSourceType.AUTO and the AUTO logic in CompositeBlockSource only switches sources when errors >= 3, by causing exactly 2 persistent errors on the current source (keeping it just below the switch threshold), preventing fallback to the healthy source and halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that BlockNodeProperties.host has @NotBlank validation but no format/IP validation, by registering a block node with a host value that is a valid non-blank string but resolves to a loopback or link-local address, causing the importer to connect to itself or an unintended local service, receive unexpected responses, and halt block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who operates a malicious block node send a gRPC stream response that is exactly stream.maxStreamResponseSize bytes but contains only a single oversized block item, causing the importer to accept the response (it is within size limits) but fail to parse the malformed item, throwing an exception that increments SourceHealth errors and eventually halts block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that getBucketName() returns an empty string when the network is 'other' (not in NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS) and bucketName is blank, causing S3StreamFileProvider to construct an S3 ListObjectsV2Request with an empty bucket name, resulting in an AWS SDK exception that propagates and halts all block file downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit a Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint (if left unsecured) to set hiero.mirror.importer.block.enabled to false, disabling the block stream source entirely and — combined with record stream being disabled — causing total cessation of block ingestion and transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker perform a SYN flood attack against the port specified in BlockNodeProperties (default 40840) of all configured block nodes, causing TCP connection establishment to fail for BlockNodeSubscriber, incrementing SourceHealth errors past 3, and halting block ingestion when all nodes are marked inactive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of a maximum constraint on stream.maxBlockItems by injecting a value of Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing BlockNodeSubscriber to allocate a buffer sized for 2 billion block items, triggering an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the importer and halts all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that BlockNodeProperties.port has @Min(0) and @Max(65535) but port 0 is a valid value, by registering a block node with port=0 (which is typically reserved/invalid for TCP connections), causing connection attempts to fail immediately, incrementing SourceHealth errors, and halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the @Valid annotation on ledger (which is nullable) to cause a validation cascade failure during application startup by injecting a partially-valid LedgerProperties object (e.g., with empty historyProofVerificationKey), causing the Spring context to fail to start and halting all block ingestion before it begins?
